name: Excellent Dark Sky Sites
milkyWayVisibility: Spectacular — zodiacal light, gegenschein, and zodiacal band clearly visible; M33 direct-vision naked-eye object
visibleObjects: Faint Milky Way detail to horizon
nearestDarkSkyReserves: Aoraki Mackenzie International Dark Sky Reserve
description: The darkest skies on Earth, found only in remote areas far from any human settlement. The Milky Way casts visible shadows, and the zodiacal light is bright enough to illuminate the landscape. The sky appears 'busy' with stars — faint stars are visible down to the very horizon. These pristine conditions are increasingly rare, found primarily in high-altitude deserts, remote oceanic islands, and designated dark sky reserves. Only about 1% of the world's population has regular access to Class 1 skies.
